story: Tanks rumbled and soldiers marched as the US 20th Army Corps advanced through France in 1944. On August 23, they entered two important towns: Fontainebleau and Melun de Seine. activity_question: Which two towns did the US 20th Army Corps enter?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important for Allied forces to liberate towns in France?

title: Pioneering Women's Education at Mount Holyoke
story: Cheers erupted as the first class of Mount Holyoke Female Seminary graduated in 1838. This groundbreaking school in South Hadley, Massachusetts, opened doors for women's higher education. On August 23, these pioneering students celebrated their hard work and determination. Their success paved the way for future generations of women to pursue knowledge and careers.
activity_question: Where was Mount Holyoke Female Seminary located?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important for women to have their own college in 1838?

title: Michael the Brave's Daring Stand
story: Swords clashed and horses thundered as Michael the Brave led his army against the mighty Ottoman forces. On August 23, 1595, the Battle of Calugareni raged in what is now Romania. Despite being outnumbered, Michael's clever tactics and bravery helped his troops hold their ground against the larger Ottoman army.
activity_question: Who led the army against the Ottoman forces?
bonus_fact: Michael the Brave is considered a national hero in Romania for his efforts to unite Romanian lands.

title: A President's Daughter Takes the Stage
story: Lights dimmed and the audience hushed as Margaret Truman stepped onto the stage. The daughter of US President Harry Truman was about to make her public singing debut. On August 23, 1947, Margaret's voice filled the Hollywood Bowl, marking the start of her musical journey. Despite her father's important job, Margaret pursued her own dreams of becoming a singer.
activity_question: Where did Margaret Truman give her first public singing concert?
follow_up_question: What was Margaret's father's job at the time of her concert?
